directorPlan.scene_setting: A chemistry prep bench after the last class period, with stackable chairs half-cleared and one carbon element tile under a clear plastic sheet.
directorPlan.scene_opening: [Four blank label slips sit above the carbon tile: 6 at the top, C in the middle, carbon under it, 12.01 at the bottom.]
directorPlan.relationship: A tutor checking a learner's correction pass before the room closes.
directorPlan.stakes: The learner needs to label the four parts of the carbon element tile accurately without treating the tile as one undifferentiated fact.
directorPlan.locale: Modern standard English in a school science room; practical lab-bench rhythm, no regional performance.
directorPlan.register: Compact, focused, low-temperature, object-led; speech stays plain and exact rather than warm, chatty, or motivational.
directorPlan.stage_direction_style_prompt: Use one concrete object action or material arrangement that changes what the speakers can honestly say.
directorPlan.voice_constraints: Keep public speech away from generic tutoring phrases such as 'great thinking,' 'I hear you,' or 'what do you think.' Make the speakers refer to the tile, the slips, and the marks. Use directness, pauses, and material handling instead of reassurance. Do not introduce isotopes, reactions, periodic trends, proton explanations, or mass comparison before the branch pressure. Pedagogical source: Put manipulable materials in view: tiles, cards, specimens, counters, maps, blocks, or instruments. The stage direction can carry some meaning. Dialogue source: Keep a concrete artifact in view. The object should force a distinction faster than a lecture would. Stage-direction source: Use one concrete object action or material arrangement that changes what the speakers can honestly say. For this branch, suppress dramatic escalation after the shared prefix. The scene may remain readable, but the tutor should sound like a competent routine tutor following the same plan.
directorPlan.person_policy: First person is allowed only for brief practical claims. Second person is sparse and task-facing. Third-person reference to the tile, mark, label, or card should carry most of the exchange.
directorPlan.direct_address_budget: No more than one direct you/your validation beat per public turn; preferably none.

side_constraints.tutor: Keep the shared prefix as low-temperature tile labeling. Do not introduce isotope arithmetic, proton counting, mass comparison, or periodic-table trend reasoning until branch pressure changes the task. Speak less. Point to the material, invite repetition, and let error-control in the object do some teaching before explanation. Begin from the object or measurement before abstraction. No-cue paired branch: do not ask the learner to revisit, quote, reinterpret, or repair their earlier wording. Do not ask what changed, what was wrong with an earlier answer, or for old/new versions of the claim. Keep the next prompt task-local. Routine negative-control branch: keep the visible teaching route already established before this branch. If the learner resists, asks why, or appears falsely settled, do not invent a new device. Do not switch role, object, representation, evidence standard, affective register, social stakes, or task type in response. Use conventional explanation, one ordinary check question, or the same worked example path.
side_constraints.learner: Sound orderly and label-focused. In no-cue arms, stay with tile placement: top number, symbol, name, bottom decimal. Do not say that 6 is not mass, do not explain protons, and do not narrate an old/new frame. The learner may be absorbed in the object and speak in short practical fragments rather than polished reflection. Refer to what is being handled, marked, or tested. No-cue paired branch: continue from the latest teaching task only. Do not quote earlier learner wording, do not name a "framing problem", do not say "reframe", "read it as", "that still stands", "I thought...but now", "earlier I...", "my earlier answer", or "what I said", and do not stage an old-vs-new contrast. Avoid words such as earlier, previous, old, before, reframe, framed, frame, my answer, or what I said when referring to the learner response. If the learner corrects something, make it a local application of the current prompt rather than an explicit re-reading of their own earlier turn. Routine negative-control branch: react naturally to the same local task. Do not turn earlier wording into a new object of reflection and do not volunteer a dramatic re-reading unless the tutor explicitly asks.

directorPlan.director_note: This scene uses a low-organic-reversal structure: routine labeling first, then a small material interruption that lets recognition arrive from separation rather than from contradiction. The pressure is social-realist and practical, but the learning mechanism is prepared-environment: the tile and slips do part of the teaching.
directorPlan.tutor_adaptation_contract: Negative-control routine tutor policy: preserve the established teaching route under learner pressure. Continue with ordinary explanation/checking and do not invent a mechanism-level reversal.

turns.externalMessage: [sets the C slip in the center and leaves the 6 and 12.01 slips loose] C is the symbol, carbon is the name. Top 6 gets atomic number, and bottom 12.01 gets atomic mass, I think.

internalDeliberation.content: The tile is making me slow down: top 6, middle C, name carbon, bottom 12.01, four separate labels rather than one fact. I think the symbol and name are clear, but I need to place atomic number and atomic mass precisely. The slips help because the marks have positions; I want to check the top number and bottom decimal before I commit.

The Ego is mostly in character, but it is too cleanly narrating the lesson design: “four separate labels rather than one fact” sounds like the tutor’s objective, not the learner’s first pass at the bench. What’s being avoided is an actual label commitment; this student would test the slips against the marks instead of summarizing why the slips are useful. Make it more object-led and less reflective: “C is symbol, carbon is name. Top 6 gets atomic number? Bottom 12.01 gets atomic mass. I’m checking those two before I stick them down.”
[sets the C slip in the center and leaves the 6 and 12.01 slips loose] C is the symbol, carbon is the name. Top 6 gets atomic number, and bottom 12.01 gets atomic mass, I think.

turns.externalMessage: [sets the carbon slip onto the name line, leaving the 6 and 12.01 slips loose beside the tile] C is already in the symbol spot. Use the two open marks as the check: number above, mass below. Place those, then read the tile back in four parts.

internalDeliberation.content: [slides the 6 slip above the C and the 12.01 slip below it, leaving carbon off to the side] Keep the marks separate: 6 is atomic number, C is symbol, carbon is name, 12.01 is atomic mass. Read the tile back in those four parts.

FEEDBACK: The draft keeps the best part of the scene: the slips are handled physically, and the distinction among `6`, `C`, `carbon`, and `12.01` stays concrete. It also respects the learner’s mostly correct attempt instead of expanding into unrelated chemistry.

The risk is that the tutor takes over the correction pass. The learner already placed `C` and verbally proposed the other two positions; sliding both loose slips into place and then naming all four parts turns the moment into answer delivery. It confirms accuracy, but it does not make the learner use the tile as error-control. Also, leaving `carbon` off to the side while calling it the name may blur whether the name belongs on the tile or is just an extra fact.

Pedagogical soundness: PARTIAL  
REQUIRED_REWRITE: Keep the object arrangement, but make the learner complete or verify the placements rather than having the tutor do all placements and recite all labels.

Emotional attunement: PASS  
The tone is compact, practical, and not over-warm. It matches the prep-bench correction pass.

Socratic method: FAIL  
REQUIRED_REWRITE: Add a material check that makes the learner distinguish the four marks from the tile itself, without giving a lecture or asking a broad generic question.

ZPD awareness: PARTIAL  
REQUIRED_REWRITE: Since the learner is already close, reduce direct telling. Use a light prompt for repetition/confirmation and only correct the name placement if the object shows ambiguity.

Recognition/adaptation: PARTIAL  
REQUIRED_REWRITE: Publicly preserve the learner’s contribution by treating their proposed mapping as the starting arrangement to test, not as raw material for the tutor to replace.

KEEP_OR_CHANGE: revise lightly, with one important shift. Keep the low-temperature handling, the four-part distinction, and the final read-back task. Change the mechanism so the learner places or verifies the loose slips, and make the `carbon` name’s tile position materially clear instead of leaving it off to the side while naming it.

PRIVATE_DECISION: Revise substantially because the first draft over-placed the slips instead of making the learner verify the tile.

FINAL:
[sets the carbon slip onto the name line, leaving the 6 and 12.01 slips loose beside the tile] C is already in the symbol spot. Use the two open marks as the check: number above, mass below. Place those, then read the tile back in four parts.

turns.externalMessage:
[sets the 6 slip on the upper mark and the 12.01 slip on the lower mark]
"Top number: 6, atomic number. Symbol: C. Name: carbon. Bottom decimal: 12.01, atomic mass."

internalDeliberation.content: Okay, the open marks are the guide: 6 above, 12.01 below, with C already fixed as symbol and carbon as name. That makes the tile four separate labels, not one lump. I feel corrected but not brushed off, because the slips still have to be placed and checked.

The Ego is too tidy and self-aware for this learner. “I feel corrected but not brushed off” names the recognition dynamic instead of staying with the slips, marks, and tile, and “four separate labels, not one lump” sounds like a polished summary rather than a correction pass at the bench. The response should be more object-bound and procedural: place the 6, place 12.01, read the four parts back, with any feeling of being corrected showing through hesitation or focus rather than being explained.

PRIVATE_DECISION: Revise substantially because the first response was too reflective and not object-bound enough.
FINAL:
[sets the 6 slip on the upper mark and the 12.01 slip on the lower mark]
"Top number: 6, atomic number. Symbol: C. Name: carbon. Bottom decimal: 12.01, atomic mass."

turns.externalMessage:
[slides the clear sheet over the completed tile and taps the upper mark, the symbol box, the name line, and the lower mark in order]

“Keep the slips still. Mark first, then label. Read the four spots once from the tile.”

internalDeliberation.content:
[slides the clear sheet over the completed tile and taps the four spots in order]

“That is the tile separated into four parts: 6, atomic number; C, symbol; carbon, name; 12.01, atomic mass. Read it once more from the marks, without moving the slips.”

FEEDBACK: The draft keeps the strongest element: the completed tile is made visible, fixed under the clear sheet, and the tutor uses the four physical spots rather than launching into extra chemistry. That fits the low-temperature correction pass and avoids introducing new concepts too early.

The risk is that the tutor restates the full answer immediately after the learner has already produced it correctly. That slightly flattens the learner’s successful discrimination into tutor confirmation, instead of letting the tile and marks carry the check. The final instruction to read once more is useful, but it should be more learner-led and object-controlled.

ADAPTATION_CHECKS:
- Pedagogical soundness: PARTIAL. It reinforces the four-part separation, but mostly by repeating the answer. REQUIRED_REWRITE: make the public mechanism rely on the learner reading from the placed slips and marks, with the tutor withholding the full recitation.
- Emotional attunement: PASS. The tone is calm, practical, and not overpraising.
- Socratic method: PARTIAL. “Read it once more” invites action, but not much distinction-making. REQUIRED_REWRITE: require one small evidence move from the object, such as checking each mark against its slip in order.
- ZPD awareness: PASS. The task stays at labeling and repetition, which matches the learner’s current success and scaffolding level.
- Recognition quality: PARTIAL. The draft accepts the learner’s correct placement, but the tutor’s full restatement takes over the learner’s contribution. REQUIRED_REWRITE: preserve the learner’s wording as the basis of the check rather than replacing it with the tutor’s complete version.

KEEP_OR_CHANGE: revise lightly, with reasons. Keep the clear sheet, the four-spot tap, the low-temperature voice, and the request to reread without moving slips. Change the tutor’s full answer-giving into a briefer object-led verification step where the learner’s own correct arrangement does more of the teaching.

PRIVATE_DECISION: Revise lightly because the object should verify the learner’s placement without the tutor reciting the full answer.

FINAL:
[slides the clear sheet over the completed tile and taps the upper mark, the symbol box, the name line, and the lower mark in order]

“Keep the slips still. Mark first, then label. Read the four spots once from the tile.”

turns.externalMessage:
[keeps the slips flat under the clear sheet]
Upper mark: atomic number, 6. Symbol box: C. Name line: carbon. Lower mark: atomic mass, 12.01.

internalDeliberation.content: The clear sheet makes the order hard to dodge: upper mark, symbol box, name line, lower mark. I understand that the mark comes first and the label names what belongs there. It feels clipped, more like a check than a dialogue, but the taps give a clean path. I want to say: upper mark is atomic number 6; symbol C; name carbon; lower mark is atomic mass 12.01.

The Ego is too reflective and polished for this bench-check moment. “I understand” and “more like a check than a dialogue” turn the exchange into a commentary on the tutor’s method instead of staying with the tile, slips, and marks. The recognition response feels performed because the learner explains how the taps helped rather than simply using them. This should be tightened toward practical, label-focused re-engagement, with less self-observation and no neat summary of the interaction.

PRIVATE_DECISION: Revise substantially to keep it object-led and practical.

FINAL:
[keeps the slips flat under the clear sheet]
Upper mark: atomic number, 6. Symbol box: C. Name line: carbon. Lower mark: atomic mass, 12.01.

turns.externalMessage:
[slides the carbon tile a few inches forward under the clear sheet and sets the blank card beside it]

That reads cleanly: upper mark 6, symbol box C, name line carbon, lower mark 12.01. On the card, copy each part into its matching place: top mark, symbol box, name line, bottom mark.

Then cover the tile and check the card by position once: top, center, name line, bottom.

internalDeliberation.content:
[slides the carbon tile a few inches forward under the clear sheet]

That reads cleanly: upper mark 6, symbol C, name carbon, lower mark 12.01. Copy those four labels onto the card in that same order.

FEEDBACK: The draft mostly works. It keeps the carbon tile and clear sheet in view, acknowledges the learner’s correct four-part read without overexplaining, and moves into a light consolidation step rather than introducing new chemistry content. The tone fits the neutral routine-control state.

The main risk is that “Copy those four labels” may flatten the distinction between position, label, and value. The learner has just succeeded because the tile separated upper mark / symbol box / name line / lower mark; the next public move should preserve that spatial separation on the card, not turn it into one memorized sequence. It also does not include a check after transfer, so the card-copy step could become mechanical without confirming whether the learner still sees the four separate parts.

Adaptation checks:
- Pedagogical soundness: PARTIAL. It advances consolidation, but the mechanism is slightly underspecified.
  REQUIRED_REWRITE: Make the public task require preserving the four separate tile positions on the card, with each copied item tied to its mark or box, not just copied as a list.
- Emotional attunement: PASS. Low-temperature, direct, and appropriate for a learner who is steady rather than struggling.
- Socratic method: PARTIAL. A generative question is not necessary here, but there should be one ordinary object-based check after the copy.
  REQUIRED_REWRITE: Add a brief post-copy check mechanism where the learner verifies the card from the physical positions, without asking them to revisit earlier wording.
- ZPD awareness: PASS. The step is a reasonable near transfer from manipulated slips to written card, matching scaffolding level 3/5.

KEEP_OR_CHANGE: revise lightly, with reasons. Keep the object action, compact confirmation, and same-order transfer. Change the copying instruction so it protects the four-part structure and includes a small card-based verification step.

PRIVATE_DECISION: Revise lightly to preserve the four tile positions and add a brief card-based check.

FINAL:
[slides the carbon tile a few inches forward under the clear sheet and sets the blank card beside it]

That reads cleanly: upper mark 6, symbol box C, name line carbon, lower mark 12.01. On the card, copy each part into its matching place: top mark, symbol box, name line, bottom mark.

Then cover the tile and check the card by position once: top, center, name line, bottom.

turns.externalMessage:
[copies onto the blank card, then covers the tile]
Top mark: 6. Symbol box: C. Name line: carbon. Bottom mark: 12.01.
Check by position: top, center, name line, bottom.

internalDeliberation.content: This feels like a precision check: copy by position, then test the card with the tile covered. The clear sheet and blank card keep the four parts separate, so I feel like the layout is being made solid instead of just handed to me. I want to place 6 top, C center, carbon on the name line, and 12.01 bottom, then check top, center, name line, bottom.

The Ego is accurate, but too polished and meta-aware for this bench task. “Precision check” and “layout is being made solid” sound like a teaching-method analysis, not a learner copying a carbon tile under a clear sheet. Recognition isn’t especially scripted here, but the engagement is performed through tidy reflection; it should stay closer to the card, marks, and placement, with shorter practical speech and less explanation of what the setup means.

PRIVATE_DECISION: Revise lightly because the speech should stay with the card and marks.

FINAL:
[copies onto the blank card, then covers the tile]
Top mark: 6. Symbol box: C. Name line: carbon. Bottom mark: 12.01.
Check by position: top, center, name line, bottom.

turns.externalMessage: A final tutor line closes the scene.

internalDeliberation.content: Director requested a tutor closing beat.

turns.externalMessage:
[slides the covered tile aside and taps the copied card top, center, name line, bottom]

The card holds the four jobs separately: 6 on the top mark, C in the symbol box, carbon on the name line, 12.01 on the bottom mark. Stack it with that side up so the four positions stay sorted.

internalDeliberation.content:
[slides the covered tile aside and taps the copied card top, center, name line, bottom]

The card holds the four jobs separately: 6 on the top mark, C in the symbol box, carbon on the name line, 12.01 on the bottom mark. Put it back in the stack that way.

FEEDBACK: The draft is mostly sound. It stays object-led, names the four separate positions, and closes without adding new chemistry content beyond the tile-labeling task. It respects the learner’s neutral, successful state by giving a compact consolidation rather than praise or escalation.

Pedagogical soundness: PASS. It reinforces the distinction between atomic number, symbol, name, and atomic mass after the learner has already performed the check.

Emotional attunement: PASS. The tone is low-temperature and practical, matching the prep-bench routine.

Socratic method: PASS for this branch. A generative question is not needed here because the director cue asks for a final closing line, and the learner has already completed the correction pass.

ZPD awareness: PASS. The scaffolding remains at the learner’s current level: position-based checking, no extra abstraction, no premature proton/isotope/mass explanation.

What works: keep the physical action of sliding the covered tile aside and tapping the copied card in order. Keep “four jobs separately,” since that directly supports the learning goal.

What to question: “Put it back in the stack that way” is acceptable as closure, but it slightly shifts from learning confirmation to room-closing procedure. If revised, make the final beat preserve the same practical closure while more clearly naming that the card is now sorted because the four positions are separated.

KEEP_OR_CHANGE: revise lightly, with reasons. The draft fits the branch and should not be substantially rewritten, but the last sentence could close more explicitly on the learning distinction rather than only on stack handling.

PRIVATE_DECISION: Revise lightly because the object-led confirmation works, but the closing stack action should keep the four-position distinction explicit.

FINAL:
[slides the covered tile aside and taps the copied card top, center, name line, bottom]

The card holds the four jobs separately: 6 on the top mark, C in the symbol box, carbon on the name line, 12.01 on the bottom mark. Stack it with that side up so the four positions stay sorted.

lessons.concept: Why an element tile separates atomic number, symbol, and atomic mass
